name: openpulse

metadata.name: openpulse
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# OpenPulse Python Reference

[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/openqasm/openqasm.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)<!-- long-description-skip-begin -->[![Release](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/openpulse)](https://pypi.org/project/openpulse)<!-- long-description-skip-end -->

OpenPulse is a concrete implementation of pulse grammar for `cal` and `defcal` body.
It reuses the classical types and statements from openqasm3.

The package is structured similarly to `openqasm3` with the modules:

* `openpulse.ast`: The AST nodes. Identical nodes from `openqasm3.ast` are re-exported.

* `openpulse.parser`: A parser similar to `openqasm3.parser`, but for OpenPulse.

* `tests`: A set of unit tests.

* No visitor module as `openqasm3.visitor` can be reused.

The OpenPulse grammar introduce the following:

* Pulse types: `port`, `frame` and `waveform`.
* Extension to OpenQASM 3 classical type to consume the pulse types.

modules.name: openpulse._antlr
documentation:
ANTLR-generated files for parsing OpenPulse files.

This package sets up its import contents to be taken from the generated files
whose ANTLR version matches the installed version of the ANTLR runtime. The
generated files should be placed in directories called `_<major>_<minor>`,
where `major` is 4, and `minor` is the minor version of ANTLR (e.g. if ANTLR
4.10 was used, those files should be in `_4_10`).

The ANTLR files from more than one version of ANTLR can be present at once. This package will
dynamically load the correct version based on the installed version of the runtime.

variables.name: openpulse._antlr.RUNTIME_VERSION
documentation: The runtime-detected version of the ANTLR runtime, as a tuple like `sys.version_info`.

classes.name: openpulse._antlr.ANTLRMetaPathFinder

modules.name: openpulse._antlr._4_11.openpulseLexer

modules.name: openpulse
documentation:
# OpenPulse Python reference package

This package contains the reference abstract syntax tree (AST) for representing
OpenPulse programs, tools to parse text into this AST, and tools to manipulate
the AST.

The AST itself is in the {obj}`.ast` module. There is a reference parser in the
{obj}`.parser` module, which requires the `[parser]` extra to be installed.

With the `[parser]` extra installed, the simplest interface to the parser is
the {obj}`~parser.parse` function.

modules.name: openpulse.ast
documentation:
# Abstract Syntax Tree (`openpulse.ast`)

```
.. currentmodule:: openpulse.ast
```

The reference abstract syntax tree (AST) for OpenPulse programs.

classes.name: openpulse.ast.WaveformType
documentation: Leaf node representing the `waveform` type.
From Pulse grammar

classes.name: openpulse.ast.PortType
documentation: Leaf node representing the `port` type.

classes.name: openpulse.ast.FrameType
documentation: Leaf node representing the `frame` type.

classes.name: openpulse.ast.CalibrationBlock
documentation: Node representing a calibration block.

classes.name: openpulse.ast.CalibrationStatement
documentation: Override the class from openqasm3

classes.name: openpulse.ast.CalibrationDefinition
documentation: Override the class from openqasm3

modules.name: openpulse.spec
documentation:
# Supported Specification Metadata (`openpulse.spec`)

```
.. currentmodule:: openpulse.spec
```

Metadata on the specifications supported by this package.

```
.. autodata:: supported_versions
```

functions.name: openpulse.printer.dump
documentation:
Write textual OpenQASM 3 code representing `node` to the open stream `file`.

It is generally expected that `node` will be an instance of {class}`.ast.Program`, but this
does not need to be the case.

For more details on the available keyword arguments, see {ref}`printer-kwargs`.

functions.name: openpulse.printer.dumps
documentation:
Get a string representation of the OpenQASM 3 code representing `node`.

It is generally expected that `node` will be an instance of {class}`.ast.Program`, but this
does not need to be the case.

For more details on the available keyword arguments, see {ref}`printer-kwargs`.

modules.name: openpulse.parser
documentation:
# Parser (`openpulse.parser`)

Tools for parsing OpenPulse programs into the {obj}`reference AST <openpulse.ast>`.

The quick-start interface is simply to call `openpulse.parse`:

```
.. currentmodule:: openpulse
```

```
.. autofunction:: openpulse.parse
```

The rest of this module provides some lower-level internals of the parser.

```
.. currentmodule:: openpulse.parser
```

```
.. autoclass:: OpenPulseNodeVisitor
```

functions.name: openpulse.parser.parse
documentation:
Parse a complete OpenPulse program from a string.

```
param input

A string containing a complete OpenQASM 3 program.

param permissive

A Boolean controlling whether ANTLR should attempt to
recover from incorrect input or not.  Defaults to False; if set to
True, the reference AST produced may be invalid if ANTLR emits any
warning messages during its parsing phase.

return

A complete  node.
```

classes.name: openpulse.parser.OpenPulseParsingError
documentation:
An error raised by the AST visitor during the AST-generation phase.  This is raised in cases where the
given program could not be correctly parsed.

classes.name: openpulse.parser.OpenPulseNodeVisitor
documentation: Base class for the visitor of the OpenPulse AST.

classes.name: openpulse.parser.CalParser
documentation:
Visit OpenQASM3 AST and parse calibration

Attributes:
  permissive: should OpenPulse parsing be permissive? If True, ANTLR
    will attempt error recovery (although parsing may still fail elsewhere).
